type: AGREE
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
region: Cache.__init__ signature+body
code:     def __init__(
        self,
        enable_disk_cache: bool,
        enable_memory_cache: bool,
        disk_cache_dir: str,
        disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = 1024 * 1024 * 10,
        memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
        ttl: Optional[int] = None,
        compress: Optional[str] = None,
        cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
        eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
            enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
            disk_cache_dir: The directory where the disk cache is stored.
            disk_size_limit_bytes: The maximum size of the disk cache (in bytes).
            memory_max_entries: The maximum size of the in-memory cache (in number of items).
            ttl: The time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
            compress: The on-disk compression codec to use. One of "gzip", "zlib", or None to disable
                compression.
            cull_limit: The maximum number of items culled from the disk cache during eviction. If None,
                the diskcache default is used.
            eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the disk cache (e.g. "least-recently-stored"). If
                None, the diskcache default is used.
        """

        self.enable_disk_cache = enable_disk_cache
        self.enable_memory_cache = enable_memory_cache
        self.ttl = ttl
        self.compress = compress
        if self.enable_memory_cache:
            if ttl is not None:
                self.memory_cache = TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl)
            else:
                self.memory_cache = LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries)
        else:
            self.memory_cache = {}
        if self.enable_disk_cache:
            disk_cache_kwargs = {
                "shards": 16,
                "timeout": 10,
                "directory": disk_cache_dir,
                "size_limit": disk_size_limit_bytes,
            }
            if cull_limit is not None:
                disk_cache_kwargs["cull_limit"] = cull_limit
            if eviction_policy is not None:
                disk_cache_kwargs["eviction_policy"] = eviction_policy
            self.disk_cache = FanoutCache(**disk_cache_kwargs)
        else:
            self.disk_cache = {}

        self._lock = threading.RLock()
note: agreed verbatim, matches my TTL logic exactly

fields.code:
def __init__(
        self,
        enable_disk_cache: bool,
        enable_memory_cache: bool,
        disk_cache_dir: str,
        disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = 1024 * 1024 * 10,
        memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
        ttl: Optional[int] = None,
        compress: Optional[str] = None,
        cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
        eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
            enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
            disk_cache_dir: The directory where the disk cache is stored.
            disk_size_limit_bytes: The maximum size of the disk cache (in bytes).
            memory_max_entries: The maximum size of the in-memory cache (in number of items).
            ttl: The time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
            compress: The on-disk compression codec to use. One of "gzip", "zlib", or None to disable
                compression.
            cull_limit: The maximum number of items culled from the disk cache during eviction. If None,
                the diskcache default is used.
            eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the disk cache (e.g. "least-recently-stored"). If
                None, the diskcache default is used.
        """

        self.enable_disk_cache = enable_disk_cache
        self.enable_memory_cache = enable_memory_cache
        self.ttl = ttl
        self.compress = compress
        if self.enable_memory_cache:
            if ttl is not None:
                self.memory_cache = TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl)
            else:
                self.memory_cache = LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries)
        else:
            self.memory_cache = {}
        if self.enable_disk_cache:
            disk_cache_kwargs = {
                "shards": 16,
                "timeout": 10,
                "directory": disk_cache_dir,
                "size_limit": disk_size_limit_bytes,
            }
            if cull_limit is not None:
                disk_cache_kwargs["cull_limit"] = cull_limit
            if eviction_policy is not None:
                disk_cache_kwargs["eviction_policy"] = eviction_policy
            self.disk_cache = FanoutCache(**disk_cache_kwargs)
        else:
            self.disk_cache = {}

        self._lock = threading.RLock()
fields.note: agreed verbatim, matches my TTL logic exactly

region: configure_cache() signature+body, dspy.cache = Cache(...) call, DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) construction
code: def configure_cache(
    enable_disk_cache: Optional[bool] = True,
    enable_memory_cache: Optional[bool] = True,
    disk_cache_dir: Optional[str] = DISK_CACHE_DIR,
    disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = DISK_CACHE_LIMIT,
    memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
    enable_litellm_cache: bool = False,
    ttl: Optional[int] = None,
    compress: Optional[str] = None,
    cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
    eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Configure the cache for DSPy.

    Args:
        enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
        enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
        disk_cache_dir: The directory to store the on-disk cache.
        disk_size_limit_bytes: The size limit of the on-disk cache.
        memory_max_entries: The maximum number of entries in the in-memory cache.
        enable_litellm_cache: Whether to enable LiteLLM cache.
        ttl: The time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
        compress: The on-disk compression codec to use. One of "gzip", "zlib", or None to disable
            compression.
        cull_limit: The maximum number of items culled from the disk cache during eviction. If None,
            the diskcache default is used.
        eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the disk cache. If None, the diskcache default is
            used.
    """
    if enable_disk_cache and enable_litellm_cache:
        raise ValueError(
            "Cannot enable both LiteLLM and DSPy on-disk cache, please set at most one of `enable_disk_cache` or "
            "`enable_litellm_cache` to True."
        )

    if enable_litellm_cache:
        try:
            litellm.cache = LitellmCache(disk_cache_dir=DISK_CACHE_DIR, type="disk")

            if litellm.cache.cache.disk_cache.size_limit != DISK_CACHE_LIMIT:
                litellm.cache.cache.disk_cache.reset("size_limit", DISK_CACHE_LIMIT)
        except Exception as e:
            # It's possible that users don't have the write permissions to the cache directory.
            # In that case, we'll just disable the cache.
            logger.warning("Failed to initialize LiteLLM cache: %s", e)
            litellm.cache = None
    else:
        litellm.cache = None

    import dspy

    dspy.cache = Cache(
        enable_disk_cache,
        enable_memory_cache,
        disk_cache_dir,
        disk_size_limit_bytes,
        memory_max_entries,
        ttl,
        compress,
        cull_limit,
        eviction_policy,
    )


def set_ttl(seconds: Optional[int]):
    """Set the time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries on the default DSPy cache.

    Args:
        seconds: The time-to-live in seconds. If None, cache entries never expire.
    """
    import dspy

    configure_cache(
        enable_disk_cache=dspy.cache.enable_disk_cache,
        enable_memory_cache=dspy.cache.enable_memory_cache,
        disk_cache_dir=dspy.cache.disk_cache.directory if dspy.cache.enable_disk_cache else DISK_CACHE_DIR,
        memory_max_entries=1000000,
        ttl=seconds,
        compress=dspy.cache.compress,
    )
note: I added set_ttl() (mine, not overlapping) after configure_cache using it. Please confirm the configure_cache signature/dspy.cache=Cache(...) call order (ttl, compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy appended after enable_litellm_cache, matching Cache.__init__ positional order) and send DRAFT for DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) construction.

fields.code:
def configure_cache(
    enable_disk_cache: Optional[bool] = True,
    enable_memory_cache: Optional[bool] = True,
    disk_cache_dir: Optional[str] = DISK_CACHE_DIR,
    disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = DISK_CACHE_LIMIT,
    memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
    enable_litellm_cache: bool = False,
    ttl: Optional[int] = None,
    compress: Optional[str] = None,
    cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
    eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Configure the cache for DSPy.

    Args:
        enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
        enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
        disk_cache_dir: The directory to store the on-disk cache.
        disk_size_limit_bytes: The size limit of the on-disk cache.
        memory_max_entries: The maximum number of entries in the in-memory cache.
        enable_litellm_cache: Whether to enable LiteLLM cache.
        ttl: The time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
        compress: The on-disk compression codec to use. One of "gzip", "zlib", or None to disable
            compression.
        cull_limit: The maximum number of items culled from the disk cache during eviction. If None,
            the diskcache default is used.
        eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the disk cache. If None, the diskcache default is
            used.
    """
    if enable_disk_cache and enable_litellm_cache:
        raise ValueError(
            "Cannot enable both LiteLLM and DSPy on-disk cache, please set at most one of `enable_disk_cache` or "
            "`enable_litellm_cache` to True."
        )

    if enable_litellm_cache:
        try:
            litellm.cache = LitellmCache(disk_cache_dir=DISK_CACHE_DIR, type="disk")

            if litellm.cache.cache.disk_cache.size_limit != DISK_CACHE_LIMIT:
                litellm.cache.cache.disk_cache.reset("size_limit", DISK_CACHE_LIMIT)
        except Exception as e:
            # It's possible that users don't have the write permissions to the cache directory.
            # In that case, we'll just disable the cache.
            logger.warning("Failed to initialize LiteLLM cache: %s", e)
            litellm.cache = None
    else:
        litellm.cache = None

    import dspy

    dspy.cache = Cache(
        enable_disk_cache,
        enable_memory_cache,
        disk_cache_dir,
        disk_size_limit_bytes,
        memory_max_entries,
        ttl,
        compress,
        cull_limit,
        eviction_policy,
    )


def set_ttl(seconds: Optional[int]):
    """Set the time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries on the default DSPy cache.

    Args:
        seconds: The time-to-live in seconds. If None, cache entries never expire.
    """
    import dspy

    configure_cache(
        enable_disk_cache=dspy.cache.enable_disk_cache,
        enable_memory_cache=dspy.cache.enable_memory_cache,
        disk_cache_dir=dspy.cache.disk_cache.directory if dspy.cache.enable_disk_cache else DISK_CACHE_DIR,
        memory_max_entries=1000000,
        ttl=seconds,
        compress=dspy.cache.compress,
    )
fields.note: I added set_ttl() (mine, not overlapping) after configure_cache using it. Please confirm the configure_cache signature/dspy.cache=Cache(...) call order (ttl, compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy appended after enable_litellm_cache, matching Cache.__init__ positional order) and send DRAFT for DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) construction.

fields.region: Cache.get() and Cache.put() -- FINAL, resolving race
fields.note: Our AGREEs crossed in transit. THIS is the version I already sent AGREE for (my message right before your last one) -- conditional expire() in the else/miss branch, gated on self.ttl is not None. It's functionally equivalent to unconditional-at-top (diskcache already lazily checks expiry on access) but matches the PR spec's exact wording ('call expire() whenever the miss path is taken, when TTL enabled') more precisely, and avoids an extra disk_cache.expire() call on every single get() when TTL isn't even configured. Let's lock this as final -- please send one more AGREE to confirm and I'll implement verbatim.
